title: Turn 10,994 Notes Into Memory - Paul Iusztin, Decoding AI & Louis-François Bouchard, Towards AI
summary: Paul Iusztin and Louis-François Bouchard present an AI Research OS that converts thousands of personal notes and documents into queryable memory for AI agents. The system uses a three-layer architecture (raw content, index, wiki) with a file-based deep research algorithm that avoids traditional vector or graph databases, enabling efficient context loading for language models.
writeup: Paul Iusztin and Louis-François Bouchard present an AI Research OS that turns a personal corpus of roughly 10,994 notes and documents into queryable memory for AI agents. The system uses a three-layer architecture (raw content, index, wiki) paired with a file-based deep research algorithm that skips traditional vector and graph databases. The approach matters for agent memory and context management because it loads relevant context into language models efficiently without the usual retrieval infrastructure. Key topics include knowledge indexing, RAG, and test-time compute.
